Title: Novel players in organogenesis and flavonoid biosynthesis in cucumber glandular trichomes

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ECERIFERUM1 homolog; wax/cutin-related lipid metabolism protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in glandular trichomes relative to non-glandular trichomes and other ovary tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing ovaries at anthesis; VIGS in seedlings; OE/RNAi phenotyping on 8 DAF fruits
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; overexpression; RNAi; RT-qPCR; SEM-based trichome density measurement
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glandular trichome development; overexpression increases GT density and RNAi/silencing decreases GT density
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCER1 was identified among glandular trichome-related genes enriched in cucumber GTs. 2. VIGS of CsCER1 significantly reduced GT density. 3. Stable overexpression of CsCER1 increased GT density, whereas RNAi knockdown decreased GT density, strongly supporting a positive role in GT organogenesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links CsCER1 mainly to GT organogenesis and epidermal lipid metabolism rather than directly to flavonoid biosynthetic flux.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate fruit spine/glandular trichome density and potentially associated protective surface traits in cucumber breeding.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ZF-HD family; MINI ZINC FINGER 3 homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as a GT-related gene enriched in GT-associated datasets
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TRSV-based VIGS in seedling petiole/leaf tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing causes shrinking secretory head and GT dysplasia, indicating a role in GT morphogenesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMIF3 was among the 67 glandular trichome-related genes. 2. VIGS efficiently reduced its transcript level. 3. Silenced plants showed shrinking secretory heads, suggesting a role in GT development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is functional but transient; authors note additional work is needed to confirm its specific role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for modifying GT morphology, though current evidence is preliminary.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included among GT-related candidate genes for validation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsGIR2 was selected from GT-related candidates for validation. 2. VIGS reduced its expression. 3. Silenced plants showed reduced glandular trichome density.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic role in cucumber GT development remains unresolved in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate locus for altering trichome density.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Primarily affects non-glandular trichome development; many NGTs arrest at raised stage while GTs are not affected
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFOCL1 was highly expressed in GT-enriched datasets. 2. VIGS reduced transcript abundance. 3. Silencing mainly impaired NGT development rather than GT development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite GT-enriched expression, functional phenotype suggests a stronger role in NGT development; included as a core organogenesis gene tested in Results.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying epidermal trichome type balance.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMBOAT1 was selected as a lipid metabolism-related GT candidate. 2. VIGS reduced gene expression. 3. Silencing reduced glandular trichome density, linking lipid metabolism to GT development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses it as a long-chain-alcohol O-fatty-acyltransferase potentially involved in wax biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering epidermal lipid-associated trichome traits.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected as the only ABC transporter among 67 GTRGs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsABCG40 was selected from GT-related candidates because of its transporter annotation. 2. VIGS efficiently reduced expression. 3. Silenced plants displayed lower GT density.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely connected to cutin/wax-related transport rather than direct flavonoid transport in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving epidermal structure-associated traits.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid entry supplying flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamate to its CoA thioester, providing 4-coumaroyl-CoA for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xintaimici (XTMC); tbh mutant and wild type for metabolite comparison
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and likely biotic/abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Substantially increased in GTs compared with ovaries without trichomes, fruit flesh, and NGTs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing ovaries at anthesis; GT vs NGT/O/F comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC transactivation with CsTBH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GT expression is positively associated with flavonoid accumulation; CsTBH directly activates its promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs4CL was strongly upregulated in cucumber GTs. 2. Its elevated expression correlated with flavonoid enrichment in GTs. 3. CsTBH bound the Cs4CL promoter in Y1H and EMSA and activated its promoter in dual-LUC assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene accession number not stated explicitly in provided text.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to boost flavonoid precursor supply in trichomes.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core flavonoid backbone leading to flavonol/anthocyanin branches
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xintaimici (XTMC); tbh mutant and wild type
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and likely stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Substantially increased in GTs relative to O, F, and NGTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC transactivation with CsTBH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GT expression is associated with flavonoid accumulation; directly activated by CsTBH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsF3H was a GT-enriched flavonoid biosynthetic gene. 2. Its expression positively associated with elevated flavonoids in GTs. 3. CsTBH bound its promoter and activated expression in reporter assays.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: B-ring hydroxylation branch toward quercetin/delphinidin-related products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant defense and likely stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Substantially increased in GTs relative to O, F, and NGTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter motif analysis; Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GT expression is positively associated with GT flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsF3'H was significantly upregulated in GTs. 2. Its expression correlated with accumulation of major GT flavonoids. 3. Although its promoter contained CsTBH motifs, Y1H did not support CsTBH binding in contrast to other tested genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Excluded from direct CsTBH target set based on Y1H results in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xintaimici (XTMC); tbh mutant and wild type
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Substantially increased in GTs relative to O, F, and NGTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GT expression is associated with accumulation of major flavonol glycosides; directly activated by CsTBH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsFLS was strongly GT enriched. 2. Its expression matched the high abundance of flavonol-derived metabolites in GTs. 3. CsTBH directly bound and activated the CsFLS promoter.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xintaimici (XTMC); tbh mutant and wild type
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Substantially increased in GTs relative to O, F, and NGTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GT expression is positively associated with delphinidin-3-O-glucoside accumulation; promoter is bound by CsTBH, though binding appears weaker
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsBZ1 was upregulated in GTs together with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. CsTBH bound the CsBZ1 promoter in Y1H and EMSA, though binding was weaker than for some other targets. 3. This supports involvement in GT anthocyanin glycoside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not included in dual-LUC figure summary text as clearly as 4CL/F3H/FLS, but promoter binding was supported.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xintaimici (XTMC); tbh mutant and wild type
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidants likely contributing to reactive oxygen species control and biotic/abiotic stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Cs4CL, CsF3H, and CsFLS; binds promoters of Cs4CL, CsF3H, CsFLS, and CsBZ1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for effector in dual-LUC; native target promoters pCs4CL, pCsF3H, pCsFLS, pCsBZ1 in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC transient expression; HPLC metabolite quantification in tbh mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a central positive regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis in cucumber GTs and also regulates GT development; loss of function reduces major GT flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central regulator linking GT organogenesis with activation of flavonoid biosynthetic genes and accumulation of major GT flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTBH is highly expressed in cucumber glandular trichomes and was previously known as a key trichome development regulator. 2. CsTBH directly binds promoters of multiple flavonoid biosynthetic genes, especially Cs4CL, CsF3H, CsFLS, and CsBZ1. 3. CsTBH activates expression of key pathway genes in dual-LUC assays. 4. The tbh mutant shows significant reductions in astragalin, isoquercitrin, and delphinidin-3-O-glucoside, demonstrating positive control of flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly state CsTBH serves as a central regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis in cucumber GTs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering trichome-associated flavonoid accumulation, but manipulation may also alter trichome morphology and development.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because CsTBH also controls trichome organogenesis, altering it may change trichome structure or density beyond metabolite effects.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing ovaries at anthesis; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GT organogenesis with likely indirect effects on specialized metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing causes elongated GT stems, indicating involvement in GT morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsWIN1-like was identified as a GT development-related AP2/ERF TF. 2. VIGS efficiently reduced its expression. 3. Silencing produced elongated GT stems, supporting a role in glandular trichome morphogenesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses likely links to wax biosynthesis and cuticle development rather than direct flavonoid pathway regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for modifying GT morphology and possibly epidermal protective traits.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect wax/cuticle traits in addition to trichomes.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GT organogenesis with likely indirect influence on GT-specialized metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing causes elongated GT stems, suggesting altered GT differentiation/morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsESR2-like was identified as a GT development-related TF. 2. VIGS reduced its expression. 3. Silenced plants showed elongated GT stems, implying a role in shaping GT morphology.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors compare it with tomato SlLFS, suggesting a conserved role in glandular trichome differentiation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering GT architecture.
